title: aster taiwanensis var. lucens
intro: Aster taiwanensis var. lucens, also known as Taiwan aster, is a plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its bright yellow and purple flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The medicinal properties of Aster taiwanensis var. lucens have been attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote tissue repair, resulting in various health benefits such as improved immune function and wound healing. Herbal preparations of Aster taiwanensis var. lucens include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
